meta.title: Solana in the Multi-Chain World by NEAR Intents

transcript: Okay. Well, thanks everyone who made it to the end of the... almost end of the day. Really excited to be here. For those who not familiar with me, I actually before jumping into blockchain rabbit hole was AI researcher. I worked at Google Research on effectively deep learning, machine learn... machine understanding. And I'm one of the co-authors of "Attention Is All You Need," which is a paper that introduced Transformers, the T in GPT. Now, in 2017, I left Google to start AI company. And we quickly realized that we actually need a blockchain so that we can coordinate and pay people around the world. Now, that's how we got to NEAR Protocol in 2018. Being obviously... we actually were neighbors with Solana in San Francisco when we were starting. And today I want to talk about kind of cross-chain and what we've been building on this side and how we've been working with Solana. So cross-chain has been extremely painful. And like if we look out, the reality is most of users have been sitting on centralized exchanges kind of historically for all of the time the blockchain existed. It's been too hard to use, you know, if I have some Bitcoin and I want some Solana, if I have some XRP and I want some NEAR, it, you know, there's gas costs, you need to figure out how to, like, which wallet to use, etc. There's a lot of risks that involve with this, you know, bridges constantly fail. It's a lot of fragmentation that, you know, you never know is like, is this bridge actually works with this chain? Do I need another bridge to kind of go somewhere, etc.? And at the end, like if you think of like Web3 wallets, right, the wallets that we actually use, for them it's really complicated to support all of these routes, all of these different bridges. It, you know, kind of adds a lot of complexity on their side. And so that's kind of been the general feel of kind of multi-chain ecosystem where every blockchain and ecosystem became an island. They've been, you know, existing on their own. And this really limited what we can do, right? And again, most users ended up just sitting on centralized exchanges and not benefiting from accessing DeFi. Now, what we brought in with NEAR Intents is effectively redefining how this experience works. We really introduce a new type of transaction, we call it intent, where you can actually express the outcome you want without needing to figure out how exact the execution gonna work, right? What you can do, you can say, hey, you know, I have some, for example, Solana, I want to buy some Zcash. I have some Bitcoin, I want USDT. And you effectively get matched with a... we call them solvers, effectively a market maker or an AI agent that is willing to take the other part. That is willing to provide this. Now, extremely important, this actually ends up very cheap because you can actually source liquidity from all of the sources, right? There can be market makers that are contra-trading on Binance. There can be some prop trading firms that are taking a position, the reverse position. There is no MEV because this contract between you and the counterparty is done off-chain. And so, now how do you actually ensure that the security of this is done, right? And this is what kind of the infrastructure that NEAR been building for past few years comes in place where we have Threshold Multi-Party Computation. This enables to actually custody assets across different chains. We have Trusted Execution Environments for verifiable and confidential computing. We use it for kind of running services as well as AI and kind of an economic incentives to deploy this. So there is a very simple API to really enable this for any wallet or an application. And we already working with a number of chains as well as number of wallets and distribution channels, right? I think we have 28 chains now. We have more coming. All the major ones already integrated. As well as, you know, Trust, Ledger, Infinex, Kyber, etc. are being using NEAR Intents to really access all of this liquidity and all of these different assets. Effectively providing a centralized exchange experience to decentralized user... kind of self-custodial users. So really exciting. It's been growing extremely... it's been doubling every month since we launched it last kind of end of last year. We just, I think, crossed 3.6 billion in last 30 days as well as on number of transactions and actions and intents we processing. Super quickly for those who are technical and want to understand how exactly it works: you specify... you kind of request what outcome you want and you effectively getting matched... like it gets distributed to market makers and solvers. They then respond that they are willing to take that trade. You effectively making, you know, a contract with them. So that is happening off-chain. So this can happen, you know, within 100-200 milliseconds. You can really quickly get to this agreement. And then you effectively settle on-chain. For example, from Solana send the assets to the address that's controlled by Multi-Party Computation. That gets recorded on NEAR. The trade gets executed and settled. And then the asset gets distributed back on, for example, Zcash to your shielded wallet into directly your private vault. Right, so this kind of experience really smooth. Kind of from a developer perspective, really easy API. And from a user perspective, a trader perspective, probably the fastest way to trade across chains directly into their self-custodial wallets. Now, intents are not just for trading, right? The trading is definitely the first use case where this is the most painful experience. But we actually this week launched here with local Abu Dhabi blockchain ADI the travel intent-driven commerce where you can actually specify natural language intent where, you know, I want to fly from Abu Dhabi to London and, you know, I'm gonna spend like three days there and I want to visit some museums. And you have now solvers, AI agents coming in and proposing you the itinerary and then also executing the actual booking and doing all the actions on the back. This also then gets extended to e-commerce, to actual contractual service work. Right, so really intents is kind of this new paradigm to find the counterparty and get agreement with them and really settle it and execute it across different spaces. Now, I'm really excited. We already working with a number of Solana projects. So Encrypt Trade actually announced it today here on stage that they using NEAR Intents to really bring privacy to other assets. And they've been using it for Zcash already on Solana. We've been working with Raydium as well where Zcash being brought to Solana and traded on Raydium. And kind of the also arbitrage and coming back to Zcash is done through NEAR Intents. And the same with Orca being really powering this. So all in all, what we trying to build is really this unified liquidity layer where you can actually plug in into any chain on any kind of asset beyond blockchains into Web2 and then really introduce this to all of the user interfaces, wallets, applications. And kind of the way we expect this kind of continue evolving is that I as a user will be able to come in with any wallet, right, be your Solana wallet or NEAR wallet, Bitcoin wallet, and use any application, right? And all of the kind of execution machinery that's gonna happen gonna be abstracted out and happen. Also the one I forgot is Sol Swap which is also a Solana specific NEAR Intents interface. You can connect with your Solana wallets and trade all of these assets in one place. So check it out. If you're a developer, this is extremely powerful infrastructure for you to use. And if you're a user trader, this is gives you access to all of the assets across the whole market. Thank you!

sources.title: NEAR’s intent layer generates $400,000 daily, so why is the L1 mainnet still 'starving'?'
sources.snippet:
- [Headlines](https://news.futunn.com/en/main)
- NEAR’s intent layer generates $400,000 daily, so why is the L1 mainnet still 'starving'?"

# NEAR’s intent layer generates $400,000 daily, so why is the L1 mainnet still 'starving'?"

[PANews](https://www.panewslab.com/zh/articles/9b15b1e2-fb73-49d3-a287-c8f10e822226)· Nov 11 10:11

[NEAR+0.04%](https://www.futunn.com/en/stock/NEAR-US) [ZCSH+2.07%](https://www.futunn.com/en/stock/ZCSH-US) [ZECP-0.23%](https://www.futunn.com/en/stock/ZECP-US) [ZECON+1.94%](https://www.futunn.com/en/stock/7028-BMS) [Zecotek Photonics Inc0.00%](https://www.futunn.com/en/stock/ZMS.H-CA)

Author: Frank, PANews

Recent activity in NEAR's intent layer (NEAR Intents) has surged, with daily trading volume soaring to over $200 million, generating fees that temporarily exceeded $400,000. Based on this data, its trading volume ranks just below Ethereum, Solana, and BSC, while its fee levels rank among the top five across all blockchains.

This 'data' boom not only brought NEAR back to the forefront of mainstream narratives after a long period of dormancy but also raised speculation: Could NEAR stage a resurgence as a result? However, what exactly are these $400,000 in 'fees'? And can they truly support NEAR's 'rise'?

### The Rise of NEAR Intents: A Data Explosion Driven by Privacy Coins

To understand this round of data explosion, it is essential first to clarify what 'NEAR Intents' actually is.

NEAR Intents (Intent Layer) is a new type of transaction infrastructure introduced by the NEAR Protocol starting in November 2024. As one of the three core technological components of NEAR’s chain abstraction architecture, NEAR Intents functions as a decentralized cross-chain system. It allows users to express desired outcomes (e.g., 'exchange Token A for Token B at the best price') without specifying technical details. Solver networks then compete to fulfill these intents in the most optimal manner, handling complex cross-chain operations in the background.

This product eliminates the need for users to be familiar with multiple chain operations, accurately calculate fees, or search for the best exchanges, as required in traditional cross-chain transactions. Additionally, it can integrate AI agents to optimize trades, greatly streamlining cumbersome cross-chain operations and reducing transaction costs.

In terms of actual performance, since its launch, NEAR Intents’ daily trading volume remained at the level of several million dollars until September this year. In October, with the surge in the privacy coin sector, significant capital flowed into ZEC. It was discovered that NEAR Intents seemed to have become the optimal channel for accessing ZEC, making ZEC trading the primary use case for NEAR Intents.

On-chain data shows that the trading volume on the ZEC chain via NEAR Intents began to surge in October, reaching $5.9 million on October 1, second only to Ethereum. By November 4, this figure had grown to $23.9 million and reached $40 million on November 7, contributing to nearly one-third of the total trading volume. Meanwhile, cross-chain trading volume on the Ethereum chain also increased significantly, jointly driving the continuous rise in NEAR Intents' transaction volume.

Among all channels, SwapKit undoubtedly stands out as the most prominent, contributing a total of $4.32 million in fees, accounting for over 70%. SwapKit is a B2B aggregator integrated into major wallets like Trust Wallet. However, these several million dollars in revenue are merely generated by the solvers behind SwapKit.

### "A booming store" versus "a deserted mall"

The rise of NEAR Intents appears to bring new hope to NEAR. Many believe that if NEAR Intents can maintain its current revenue level and adopt a model similar to Hyperliquid to return or repurchase tokens using these fees, it would greatly improve the current economic narrative surrounding the NEAR token.

However, this seems unrealistic.

Firstly, from a relational per

This directly visualizes the core problem described in the transcript, where blockchains are 'islands' and cross-chain interaction is 'painful' and 'fragmented.' The high-tech, bioluminescent connection represents the new, seamless infrastructure of NEAR Intents, bypassing old, risky 'bridges.' For the homepage portrait, I included the text '$3.6B' as a holographic data point within the energy flow to quantify the story's impact—the massive monthly volume being unified by this new technology.

The background is a deep, digital abyss with faint, distant data grids, creating immense depth.

editorial.headline: NEAR Intents Integrates Solana to Unify Cross-Chain Liquidity
editorial.standfirst: NEAR Co-founder Illia Polosukhin unveils deep integrations with Solana DeFi staples, utilizing chain abstraction to bypass traditional bridging risks. This infrastructure layer allows Solana users to access Bitcoin and Zcash liquidity natively, processing over $3.6 billion in monthly volume.
key_takeaways: Encrypt Trade announced a live integration on stage to bring privacy assets like Zcash directly to Solana.
The intent-based architecture is currently processing $3.6 billion in transaction volume every 30 days.
Solana DEX leaders Raydium and Orca are leveraging NEAR’s solver network for cross-chain arbitrage and liquidity.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The fragmentation of liquidity across Layer 1 blockchains has long been the industry's primary bottleneck, forcing institutional and retail users to rely on vulnerable bridges or centralized exchanges. **NEAR Protocol** is addressing this via "Chain Abstraction," effectively positioning its network as a backend settlement layer rather than a direct competitor to high-performance chains like Solana. By utilizing **NEAR Intents**, the protocol employs a network of market makers and AI agents—termed "solvers"—to execute trades off-chain in under 200 milliseconds before settling them on-chain via Multi-Party Computation (MPC).

For the Solana ecosystem, the implications are immediate and structural. During the keynote, **Encrypt Trade** was announced as a new integration, specifically leveraging NEAR’s infrastructure to bring privacy-preserving assets like **Zcash** directly into Solana wallets. This mechanism is already active within Solana's deep liquidity venues; **Raydium** and **Orca** are utilizing the intent layer to source external liquidity, allowing for tighter spreads and seamless arbitrage opportunities without the latency and security risks associated with traditional wrapping and bridging mechanisms.

This shift represents a maturity in DeFi architecture where user experience is decoupled from the underlying chain. The volume metrics support this thesis: the intent layer has surged to **$3.6 billion** in 30-day volume, doubling month-over-month across 28 supported chains. By abstracting the complexity of gas fees and chain switching, NEAR is betting that the future of interoperability lies not in connecting chains, but in erasing the boundaries between them entirely.
